lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੁ ਨ ਗਲੀਈ ਢੂਢੀਐ ਕਥਨਾ ਕਰੜਾ ਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: giaan na galeeee dtoodteeai kathanaa kararraa saar |
lines.english: Wisdom cannot be found through mere words. To explain it is as hard as iron.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਤਾ ਪਾਈਐ ਹੋਰ ਹਿਕਮਤਿ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਖੁਆਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: karam milai taa paaeeai hor hikamat hukam khuaar |2|
lines.english: When the Lord bestows His Grace, then alone it is received; other tricks and orders are useless. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਜੇ ਆਪਣੀ ਤਾ ਨਦਰੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: nadar kareh je aapanee taa nadaree satigur paaeaa |
lines.english: If the Merciful Lord shows His Mercy, then the True Guru is found.
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਜੀਉ ਬਹੁਤੇ ਜਨਮ ਭਰੰਮਿਆ ਤਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਸੁਣਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: ehu jeeo bahute janam bharamiaa taa satigur sabad sunaaeaa |
lines.english: This soul wandered through countless incarnations, until the True Guru instructed it in the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਜੇਵਡੁ ਦਾਤਾ ਕੋ ਨਹੀ ਸਭਿ ਸੁਣਿਅਹੁ ਲੋਕ ਸਬਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: satigur jevadd daataa ko nahee sabh suniahu lok sabaaeaa |
lines.english: There is no giver as great as the True Guru; hear this, all you people.
and instructs us in the Truth of Truths. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai sach paaeaa jinaee vichahu aap gavaaeaa |
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, the True Lord is found; He removes self-conceit from within,
Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਹਣੇ ਪਉਣੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੁ ਚੰਦੁ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਅਵਤਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: gahane paun paanee baisantar chand sooraj avataar |
lines.english: The wind, water and fire are the ornaments; the sun and moon are the incarnations.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘੜੀਆ ਸਭੇ ਗੋਪੀਆ ਪਹਰ ਕੰਨੑ ਗੋਪਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: gharreea sabhe gopeea pehar kana gopaal |
lines.english: All the hours are the milk-maids, and the quarters of the day are the Krishnas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੁਸੈ ਗਿਆਨ ਵਿਹੂਣੀ ਖਾਇ ਗਇਆ ਜਮਕਾਲੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak musai giaan vihoonee khaae geaa jamakaal |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, without divine knowledge, one is plundered, and devoured by the Messenger of Death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲੀ ਧਰਤੀ ਮਾਲੁ ਧਨੁ ਵਰਤਣਿ ਸਰਬ ਜੰਜਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: sagalee dharatee maal dhan varatan sarab janjaal |
lines.english: All of the earth, property, wealth and articles are all entanglements.
First Mehl:
They move their feet and roll their heads.
The dust flies and falls upon their hair.
The disciples play the music, and the gurus dance.
They beat the drums for the sake of bread.
Beholding them, the people laugh, and then go home.
They throw themselves upon the ground.
They sing of the milk-maids, they sing of the Krishnas.
The Lord is fearless and formless; His Name is True.
They sing of Sitas, and Ramas and kings.
The entire universe is His Creation.
Contemplating the Guru, I have been taught these teachings;
The night of their lives is cool with dew; their minds are filled with love for the Lord.
Those servants, whose destiny is awakened, serve the Lord.
the numerous, countless whirlwinds in the desert,
granting His Grace, He carries His servants across.
The oil-press, the spinning wheel, the grinding stones, the potter's wheel,
the spinning tops, the churning sticks, the threshers,
the breathless tumblings of the birds,
O Nanak, the tumblers are countless and endless.
and the men moving round and round on spindles
The Lord binds us in bondage - so do we spin around.
According to their actions, so do all people dance.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਚਿ ਨਚਿ ਹਸਹਿ ਚਲਹਿ ਸੇ ਰੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: nach nach haseh chaleh se roe |
lines.english: Those who dance and dance and laugh, shall weep on their ultimate departure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਨੑ ਮਨਿ ਭਉ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak jina man bhau tinaa man bhaau |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, those whose minds are filled with the Fear of God, have the love of God in their minds as well. ||2||
They dance and jump around on the urgings of their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਡਿ ਨ ਜਾਹੀ ਸਿਧ ਨ ਹੋਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: audd na jaahee sidh na hohi |
lines.english: They do not fly to the heavens, nor do they become Siddhas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਉ ਤੇਰਾ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਨਾਇ ਲਇਐ ਨਰਕਿ ਨ ਜਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: naau teraa nirankaar hai naae leiai narak na jaaeeai |
lines.english: Your Name is the Fearless Lord; chanting Your Name, one does not have to go to hell.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਸਭੁ ਤਿਸ ਦਾ ਦੇ ਖਾਜੈ ਆਖਿ ਗਵਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: jeeo pindd sabh tis daa de khaajai aakh gavaaeeai |
lines.english: Soul and body all belong to Him; asking Him to give us sustenance is a waste.
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਰਵਾਣਾ ਪਰਹਰੈ ਜਰੁ ਵੇਸ ਕਰੇਦੀ ਆਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: je jaravaanaa paraharai jar ves karedee aaeeai |
lines.english: Even if you remove the signs of old age, old age shall still come in the guise of death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਲੋੜਹਿ ਚੰਗਾ ਆਪਣਾ ਕਰਿ ਪੁੰਨਹੁ ਨੀਚੁ ਸਦਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: je lorreh changaa aapanaa kar punahu neech sadaaeeai |
lines.english: If you yearn for goodness, then perform good deeds and feel humble.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਸਲਮਾਨਾ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਸਰੀਅਤਿ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: musalamaanaa sifat sareeat parr parr kareh beechaar |
lines.english: The Muslims praise the Islamic law; they read and reflect upon it.
No one remains here when the count of the breaths is full. ||5||
Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੰਦੇ ਸੇ ਜਿ ਪਵਹਿ ਵਿਚਿ ਬੰਦੀ ਵੇਖਣ ਕਉ ਦੀਦਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bande se ji paveh vich bandee vekhan kau deedaar |
lines.english: The Lord's bound servants are those who bind themselves to see the Lord's Vision.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿੰਦੂ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਸਾਲਾਹਨਿ ਦਰਸਨਿ ਰੂਪਿ ਅਪਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: hindoo saalaahee saalaahan darasan roop apaar |
lines.english: The Hindus praise the Praiseworthy Lord; the Blessed Vision of His Darshan, His form is incomparable.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗੀ ਸੁੰਨਿ ਧਿਆਵਨੑਿ ਜੇਤੇ ਅਲਖ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jogee sun dhiaavana jete alakh naam karataar |
lines.english: The Yogis meditate on the absolute Lord there; they call the Creator the Unseen Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥਿ ਨਾਵਹਿ ਅਰਚਾ ਪੂਜਾ ਅਗਰ ਵਾਸੁ ਬਹਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: teerath naaveh arachaa poojaa agar vaas bahakaar |
lines.english: They bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, making offerings of flowers, and burning incense before idols.
